codeReview.summary: Feature behavior aligns with VAL-BLOG-POST-001..007: valid blog routes render Markdown/MDX content and metadata, reading-time fallback is wired through shared logic, syntax highlighting is configured with Shiki, and invalid slugs resolve to explicit not-found UI. One non-blocking styling issue was found in post-content selector scoping.

issues.description: The post-body selectors are scoped as `.post-content :where(...)`, which Astro compiles to descendants requiring the component scope attribute. Rendered Markdown/MDX nodes from `<Content />` do not carry that attribute, so these rules do not apply as intended (see compiled selector in `dist/blog/shipping-a-minimal-portfolio/index.html:2` versus rendered nodes like `<h2>`/`<ul>`/`<pre>` at lines 8/10/17). Consider using `:global(...)` for post-content descendants.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission guidance does not currently document Astro scoped-style behavior for rendered Markdown/MDX (`<Content />`) descendants, which can silently bypass intended post-body styling unless `:global` is used.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker implemented scoped selectors in `src/pages/blog/[slug].astro:75-100`; compiled output shows scope-qualified descendant selectors (`dist/blog/shipping-a-minimal-portfolio/index.html:2`) while rendered Markdown nodes lack scope attributes (`.../index.html:8,10,17`).

summary: Pass. Core user-visible requirements for post rendering, metadata, fallback reading time, syntax highlighting, overflow behavior, and invalid-slug not-found semantics are implemented. One non-blocking CSS scoping issue affects intended post-body styling coverage.
